arguments.task: Research Freshdesk for agent-toolkit buildability using authoritative first-party sources. Prioritize official developer docs, auth docs, pricing/access pages, API reference, rate-limit docs, Freshworks/Freshdesk support docs, official GitHub pages if any, and official MCP registry/GitHub evidence if available. Extract only verifiable facts for: one-line description of product, API auth methods, whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/admin approval/partner-gated and why, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth, whether an MCP exists, buildability verdict, main blocker if not ready_today, rate limits, notable signals, and evidence URLs. If a field cannot be verified, note ambiguity explicitly. Use official pages over third-party sources. Visit Freshdesk developer API auth page, Freshdesk pricing page, any API overview/rate limit pages, and search official GitHub/registry for MCP evidence.

input.thought: Launch browser research on official Freshdesk sources and MCP evidence.

raw_result.description: Cloud-based customer support and helpdesk software for managing tickets, conversations, and support workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Freshdesk API docs indicate API access via a personal API key and also describe OAuth 2.0 app flows; the gathered browser evidence explicitly confirmed personal API key auth, while OAuth support should be human-verified against the current auth docs.
raw_result.gating_reason: Freshdesk pricing is self-serve and the official API docs note trial accounts have a default API rate limit, indicating API access is available on self-serve trial accounts without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public Freshdesk API v2 documented over HTTPS/JSON with standard CRUD endpoints across core helpdesk resources; breadth appears substantial but was not exhaustively enumerated from gathered evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Freshdesk MCP server or registry entry was verified from gathered evidence. Absence is not definitive and should be human-checked if critical.

raw_result.blocker: Core REST API appears public and self-serve, but some key fields remain partially unverified from the gathered evidence set alone, especially current OAuth status and authoritative MCP absence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates plan-based API rate limits, with trial accounts defaulting to 50 calls per minute. Additional per-plan details were not fully verified from the evidence already gathered.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer portal exposes Freshdesk API v2 and rate-limit documentation, suggesting a mature public integration surface. Pricing appears self-serve. Browser-gathered evidence also indicated JSON over HTTPS and standard CRUD semantics.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The gathered evidence strongly supports public REST API and self-serve access, but some fields remain ambiguous without further verification: whether OAuth 2.0 is currently documented for Freshdesk itself, exhaustive API breadth, and whether an official MCP exists versus simply not being found.
